topic: Why narcissists are so charming at first

script.title: Why Narcissists Charm You Before They Destroy You

scenes.narration: The most charming person you've ever met was probably scanning you for weakness.
scenes.image_prompt: A cracked ornate mirror reflecting only shadow, heavy velvet drapes, single dim spotlight, black and white film noir, high contrast, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Narcissists aren't warm. They're studying you. Your compliments. Your wounds. Your desperate need to feel special. They take notes while you talk.
scenes.image_prompt: An open leather journal on a mahogany desk, fountain pen poised above blank page, single overhead spotlight, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Within minutes they mirror you. Same opinions. Same taste. Same childhood pain. You feel finally seen. It's a performance.
scenes.image_prompt: A still dark pool of water reflecting only blackness, dripping ripples, empty stone walls, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Psychologists call this love bombing. Overwhelming early affection floods your brain with dopamine. You mistake intensity for intimacy. You mistake obsession for love.
scenes.image_prompt: Crumbling rose petals scattered across an empty candlelit dinner table, two untouched wine glasses, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: Then comes the test. A small cruelty. A subtle insult. They watch how you react. If you forgive too fast, they know you'll forgive everything later.
scenes.image_prompt: A single black chess piece being pushed across a marble board by a long shadow, tense close-up, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: The charm was never about you. It was about access. Your trust. Your time. Your money. Your self-image. Narcissists don't fall in love. They acquire.
scenes.image_prompt: An empty gilded theater mask resting on a velvet pedestal, dust motes in a narrow beam of light, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: By the time you see it, the mask is already off. And the person who adored you last month looks at you like a stranger.
scenes.image_prompt: A heavy wooden door slowly closing, a thin sliver of cold light disappearing through the crack, black and white film noir, no people, no faces

scenes.narration: The next time someone feels too perfect too fast, ask yourself one question. Are they seeing you? Or are they studying you?
scenes.image_prompt: A solitary hand-shaped void reaching toward a fogged mirror showing no reflection, dim lamp, black and white film noir, no people, no faces
